definition: Sovereign stress follows a consistent historical pattern across 11 episodes: term premium rise, curve steepening, FX depreciation, equity underperformance, rating downgrades, acute crisis (if at all). US currently at stage 1-2. Conditional probability of progression from stage 2 to stage 5+ is ~20-30% for monetary-sovereign reserve-currency issuers.
evidence: 20-30% conditional progression probability for reserve-currency issuers
~50-60% progression probability in full sample including non-reserve-currency issuers
open_questions: What distinguishes episodes that stop at stage 1-2 from those that progress?
Is the transition from gradual to acute stress a phase change (discontinuous) or tipping point (continuous but nonlinear)?
What specific catalyst triggers progression beyond stage 2?
